paper_title: Sedimentological and Cryostratigraphic Analysis in Delta Regions

methods.description: Collection of sedimentological logs, geomorphological observations, and cryostratigraphic and sedimentological analysis from river and coastal sections in the delta over three summer field campaigns.

steps.description: Data used for this study consists of sedimentological logs from nine river and coastal sections in the delta, geomorphological observations, and cryostratigraphic and sedimentological analysis of two 20 m long, ice-bonded sediment cores.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sedimentological logs and geomorphological observations

steps.description: Field data was collected over three summer field campaigns, in 2012, 2013 and 2015.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field campaigns for data collection

methods.description: Drilling of boreholes and collection of ice-bonded sediment cores for analysis.

steps.description: Two 20 m deep boreholes (C1 and C2) were drilled in September 2012 using a drill rig equipped with a 42 mm core barrel.
steps.searchable_summary: drill boreholes and collect sediment cores

steps.description: Retrieved cores were sealed in sterile plastic bags and stored in a freezer onsite.
steps.searchable_summary: store sediment cores in sterile conditions

methods.description: Analysis of sediment cores in the laboratory to determine cryostructures, excess ice content, and gravimetric ice content.

steps.description: In the laboratory, the C1 and C2 were split lengthwise, described and photographed.
Cryostructures were classified using a system adapted from Murton (2013) and French and Shur (2010).
Excess ice content and gravimetric ice content was calculated for ca. 180 samples.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate excess and gravimetric ice content

methods.description: Dating of sediment samples using OSL to determine the age of sand-sized quartz and feldspar grains.

steps.description: Optically Stimulated Luminescence (OSL) dating was performed on 14 samples from depths of up to 12 m on sediment from C1 and C2.
steps.searchable_summary: perform OSL dating on sediment samples

steps.description: Samples were processed at the Nordic Centre for Luminescence Dating at the University of Aarhus.
